title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After piquing the interest of Inspector Spence, a retired high-ranking official from the Criminal Investigation Department, Poirot departs to meet Mrs. Oliver at a Georgian-style red-brick house named "Apple Orchard." Poirot intends to use Spence’s connections to the local police to gather information and plans to involve Spence's sister, Elspeth, who is well-acquainted with the local community.

Upon arriving at Apple Orchard, Poirot meets Mrs. Oliver. He explains his decision to stay in a hotel rather than at Miss Judith Butler's house, noting that a detached perspective allows for more comprehensive observation. Poirot outlines his investigative plan: he intends to examine the crime scene of the thirteen-year-old girl's death, speak with the victim's mother, meet with the local inspector, the local doctor, and the school headmaster. He also schedules a meeting for six o'clock at the Spence house to discuss the case with Spence and his sister.

Mrs. Oliver introduces Poirot to Mrs. Dreyfus, a tall, athletic, and efficient woman in her mid-forties who organized the social event where the murder occurred. Poirot observes that Mrs. Dreyfus appears to be suppressing emotions, suspecting she feels a sense of inadequacy or anger that a tragedy occurred during an event she arranged. Mrs. Dreyfus greets Poirot and expresses her hope that he can help, while noting the local stir caused by the child's death and commenting on the increasing frequency of such crimes in the countryside.
